title: The Journey of Shri Dinesh Baliram Sawai, Our Founder
content: A visionary educationist and community leader from Wardha, Shri Dinesh Baliram Sawai dedicated his life to making quality education accessible to the most underserved communities of Vidarbha. In 1973 he founded Chetana Education Society — and over the following decades built it from a single initiative into a network of 7 institutions and programmes serving thousands of students annually. His belief was simple: education is not a privilege, it is a right. That belief remains the foundation of everything Chetana Education Society does today.
whoWeAre: Founded in 1973 by Shri Dinesh Baliram Sawai in Wardha, Maharashtra, Chetana Education Society has served over 35,000 students across five decades. We run schools, colleges, ITI skill training, a pharmacy institute, and a free boys hostel — bringing quality education to first-generation learners across rural Vidarbha.

ourStory.paragraph1: Chetana Education Society was founded on April 30, 1973 by educationist and social reformer Shri Dinesh Baliram Sawai in Wardha, Maharashtra — with a conviction that quality education is the most lasting instrument of human dignity and community development.
ourStory.paragraph2: Over five decades, we have built a comprehensive educational network across Wardha district — one of Maharashtra's most underserved agricultural regions. From primary schooling to professional degrees, from teacher training to industrial skills, from pharmacy education to a free residential hostel — we have consistently expanded our reach to ensure that geography and economic background are never barriers to learning.
ourStory.paragraph3: More than 35,000 students have passed through our institutions since 1991. The majority are first-generation learners from rural families — young people for whom a Chetana institution was the door to a better life. When you educate one person from an underserved family, you transform an entire household. At scale, you transform a community.
